content: The 2024-2025 cutting-edge advancements in state-of-the-art active learning agent memory can be reframed as the structural "backbone" that resolves a core limitation of curiosity-driven general agents: the tendency to forget previously explored surprising patterns and repeatedly re-investigate low-value novelty. By structuring agent memory to proactively prioritize tagging and retaining surprising, high-information interactions identified during active learning cycles, general agents can avoid novelty loops and accumulate increasingly complex world knowledge over time.
